explanation:
**Health reference values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They're guidelines; your personal medical history always takes priority.

## How It's Calculated

Does coffee dehydrate? This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside normal limits.

## Normal Reference Ranges

| Parameter | Normal Adult Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C (97-99°F) |
| Oxygen Saturation (SpO2) | 95-100% |
| Fasting Blood Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL Cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL Cholesterol | >40 (Men) / >50 (Women) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a general reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically for accuracy.

faq.a: No, this is a myth. The diuretic effect of caffeine is mild. Net effect: coffee hydrates you.

faq.q: Is tea as dehydrating as coffee?
faq.a: Similar to coffee. Tea hydrates you and provides additional antioxidants.
Yes. Alcohol is a strong diuretic and does cause dehydration.

faq.q: What counts toward my daily hydration?
faq.a: Coffee, tea, juice, and other beverages all count. But pure water is best for overall hydration.

faq.q: How much water should I drink per day?
faq.a: A common guideline is 6-8 glasses (about 2 liters) per day, though individual needs vary.
During intense exercise, long workouts, or when sick. Otherwise, normal food provides sufficient electrolytes.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about hydration?
faq.a: If values are outside normal ranges, if you have hydration-related symptoms, or if you have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic disease.

faq.q: Can I use coffee instead of water?
faq.a: Coffee hydrates, but water is the best choice. Vary your fluid intake with water, tea, and coffee.
